customers.name: The Cabinet Face
customers.evidence_quote: UX never lent to being a direct to consumer system. As Allmoxy grew our goals changed but Kyles goals remained the same. 9 months ago Jess and Kyle changed gears to make the system work well for internal estimates. Last 6 months sales have tanked so theres no need for the system. Wanted 3D but it's too clunky and doesn't work well in his eyes. [Retention call 11/22/24]
signals: Apr 2026: 'aren't paying and haven't logged in in 2 years'
customers.recommended_action: Set HubSpot Churn Reason: 'Features; Business Model Change'

customers.name: Reid MFG LLC
customers.evidence_quote: Note volume exceeded MCP response token limit (90K chars) — full notes available in HubSpot UI for human review.
signals: 20+ notes exist but couldn't be inlined in this pass
customers.recommended_action: Manual review in HubSpot — high-$ account warrants 10 minutes from CSM

customers.name: Premier Custom Millwork & Surfaces, Inc
customers.suggested_reason: Failed Implementation; Paid and Never Used
customers.evidence_quote: Bandwidth - Jesse has tried to implement Allmoxy Since October of 2022 and hasn't been able to get there partially due to his own lack of time. He recognizes this falls on him. Due to the $150/per hour service fees, the time needed to build out the rest of our product lines and the limitations in flexibility of pricing, we will be terminating our services with Allmoxy. Total Paid (Oct 22'-Dec 24') Subscription $23,624 Services $16,911 — still never got any value from utilizing the Allmoxy system.
customers.recommended_action: Set HubSpot Churn Reason: 'Failed Implementation; Paid and Never Used'

customers.name: Martin Wood Products
customers.evidence_quote: When he first started it was just gonna be a door and molding shop, was a great fit for that. Now they are 70% high end residential cabinetry, appliances, windows, and complex exterior door systems. He is afraid it isn't going to be the long-term solution. He can't get 250K worth of a job out of our designer (CV has those). [Churn risk note 10/30/24]
signals: Cabinet Vision identified as the alternative
customers.recommended_action: Set HubSpot Churn Reason: 'Features; Business Model Change'

customers.name: Stair Fittings, Inc.
customers.evidence_quote: Renewal 2024: Using Allmoxy as a bidding tool... Allmoxy doesn't generate a part number which they need for quickbooks tracking. It would be a herculean effort for back end programming that it wouldn't be worth the squeeze. Doesn't have plans to get the catalog done and rounded out this year. Could be doing $200k-$250k in revenue per month if they fully were to launch within the system. [Feb 2024]
signals: 7+ years using only as a bidding tool, never fully launched
Massive untapped potential ($200K+ MRR if launched)
customers.recommended_action: Set HubSpot Churn Reason: 'Features; Catalog Unidentified' — long-tenure account that never reached value capture

customers.name: Southern Idaho Cabinets LLC
customers.evidence_quote: Churn risk 12/31/24. Last login 10/9/24. Good amount of orders but stopped in October. Payment failed.
signals: Was actively running orders until Oct 2024
Payment failed and stopped logging in same month
Card update attempt earlier in year (Aug 2024)
customers.recommended_action: Set HubSpot Churn Reason: 'Payment Failure' — involuntary churn; possible win-back candidate

customers.name: Cabaxis
customers.evidence_quote: Only 2 notes on file: 'I had a conf. call with Brian, Jamie and Phil. I reiterated what had already been discussed in email. They are thinking about how serious they are in building the bridge.' [Jan 2019]
signals: High lifetime $ but no churn investigation done
customers.recommended_action: Manual review — high-$ account with no recent CSM notes is itself a process finding

customers.name: Timeless Cabinetry
customers.evidence_quote: No notes returned from HubSpot.
signals: Zero notes on HubSpot record despite 7+ year tenure and $19K lifetime
customers.recommended_action: Manual review — long-tenure / high-$ account with NO notes is a major process finding

customers.name: Egr Construction Inc
customers.evidence_quote: HubSpot company record exists but contains zero notes/emails/tasks. No CSM-recorded engagement.
customers.recommended_action: Long-tail customer with no CSM notes. Defer manual review or accept Unresponsive as default.

customers.name: Pinnacle Cabinet & Millwork
customers.evidence_quote: Pinnacle Cabinets originally intended to use Allmoxy for both custom cabinets and door line sales. The cabinet use case was abandoned early due to the complexity of custom work. The door line has not generated anticipated business... Market feedback suggests a lack of fit between Allmoxy's order-based model and Pinnacle's custom workflows. Pinnacle's customers (builders, contractors) typically send floorplans and expect design work, not to self-service orders online. [Retention meeting 11/19/25]
signals: 11/19/25 retention meeting documented model misfit (custom-cabinet workflow vs Allmoxy order-based)
Door line sales did not materialize as anticipated (originally 2-3M tier)
Customer requested to cancel after team discussion despite rate hold offer
customers.recommended_action: Accept — non-preventable. Their customers' floorplan-based workflow fundamentally doesn't fit Allmoxy's self-service order model.

customers.name: Organizetosize
customers.suggested_reason: Timing Not Right; Paid and Never Used
customers.evidence_quote: Just too busy between the two businesses... Lots of changes (ups and downs)... Juggling the two businesses (design to size & organize to size)... Haven't been able to run a marketing campaign yet. [Renewal notes 7/31/24]
signals: Owner overwhelmed by parallel business (Design to Size + Organize to Size)
Could not run marketing campaign to drive Allmoxy revenue
Some Etsy/B2B orders but never reached anticipated volume
customers.recommended_action: Bandwidth-constrained owner — likely not preventable by us, but watch for similar profile in qualification (single-owner side businesses).

customers.name: KIB Direct
customers.evidence_quote: Notes on file are all from 2019-2020 covering 3D image/catalog setup and color asset handoff with Derek. No churn-era CSM notes found.
signals: Long tenure (7.3 years on books) with no recent engagement
customers.recommended_action: Pre-CSM-era churn — accept Unresponsive as default, or interview Beau/Brady if account was historically significant.

customers.name: Prodigy Cabinetry
customers.evidence_quote: Had a blunt conversation with Tod that these will be the terms remaining after his next payment of $1856 on July 14. Instance fee will be $250/USD until January 14 2022 where he will return to the payment of $1856 USD per month for the remainder of the contract... [7/7/21] | Cancellation Fee Paid 03/7/2022 | QB invoice contract cancellation cost - Prodigy Cabinetry $4,600. [2/25/22]
signals: Rate negotiation conflict — temporarily dropped from $1856 to $250/mo for 6 months
Customer cancelled contract early and paid $4,600 cancellation fee
Blunt-conversation note suggests pricing was the persistent friction
customers.recommended_action: Pricing-driven churn under prior contract terms. Confirm current pricing rationale is more graduated for high-MRR shops in similar tier.

customers.name: Wrightcraft Closets
customers.evidence_quote: Potential churn risk note 10/30/24: Launched. Looks like they just use Allmoxy as a bidding system. Apparently they want a lower monthly cost but they are only paying $250/m. | 2022 note: these guys haven't done revenue in a year... moved to CS/retention.
signals: Customer used Allmoxy purely as a bidding tool, not for production orders
Already at floor pricing ($250/mo) yet still considered too high
Years with zero revenue through the system per CSM
customers.recommended_action: Block bidding-only usage at floor pricing in qualification — they consume support without value capture.

customers.name: Cooper Enterprises, Inc.
customers.evidence_quote: Spoke with Bryson and he feels that Allmoxy is not the best fit for them right now. Going to reengage in about 6 months [4/2/25] | Used us previously for fine doors only. Changing to closet systems through different configurator. Order entry system. [12/6/24]
signals: Customer explicitly stated 'not the best fit'
Moved closet line to a different configurator
Used Allmoxy previously for door-only — narrowing usage pattern
customers.recommended_action: Track the competing closet configurator. Re-engage in Q4 2025 per Bryson's '6 month' window.

customers.name: Cober Cabinets
customers.evidence_quote: Cober is currently in legal proceedings with the company and their card began failing to run in January. Due to the legality of the situation, I won't be pursuing a card on file. [4/25/24]
signals: Active legal proceedings against the customer/company
Card began failing January 2024 — pre-cancellation pattern
Prior dispute: 'She insists our software was misrepresented on big projects' (Dec 2022) but resolved to 85% fit
customers.recommended_action: Accept — legal/financial distress drove cancellation. Non-preventable.

customers.name: Custom Pro Millwork
customers.evidence_quote: Norman replied saying he wants to cancel: 'Yes. I requested in the past and no one responded. I also called every phone number I have and no one answered or called back. The shame is I really wanted this catalog and no one even bothered to follow up...' [8/3/23] | Norman is unresponsive and when he does, expresses a lot of frustration. Likely to churn. Left off with him needed to review Juans work after meeting to try and save account and attempted cancelation. [2/27/24 CS Playbook]
signals: Customer's primary complaint was Allmoxy's responsiveness/delivery — 'great product but delivery is not there'
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — process compliance failure on our side. Notes corroborate failed implementation + poor responsiveness.

customers.name: Rosehill Woodcrafters Ltd
customers.evidence_quote: This customer is officially cancelling for the time being. I have spent multiple meetings with them trying to save it but they aren't interested at the time being to make it priority and we all know this thing is not going to build itself. They also don't want to invest the resources to have a 3rd part do it. [11/10/21]
signals: Customer would not invest internal resources to complete catalog/setup
Refused to engage a third-party services build
Stalled in implementation — never reached launch
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — onboarding gate (90-day launch milestone) would have closed this earlier.

customers.name: Scherr's Cabinet and Doors, Inc.
customers.evidence_quote: $499/month. 12 month contract. 1-3 Million in revenue. Legacy discount B2B Door shop. $799/Month before discount. [3/2/22 demo notes] | Broc Hill had the instance for a year but not touched it. Maybe sell some services. [4/4/20]
signals: Had the instance over a year without touching it (2020 note)
Legacy discount applied — long-tenured but inactive
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — same onboarding gate as Rosehill / Pinnacle. Long instance dormancy is a strong early-warning signal.

customers.name: Qualita Services - Forma Cabinets
customers.evidence_quote: Cancellation Playbook: Close/Lost Reason: Not Able to Set Up. Not using the system. [3/4/24]
signals: Cancellation playbook explicitly recorded 'Not Able to Set Up'
10 weeks of onboarding meetings (Jan-March 2024) — heavy implementation effort
Catalog/pricing complexity persisted through multiple Juan/Aaron passes
Tirssa (POC) repeatedly broke pricing during catalog editing
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — POC lacked technical depth to maintain catalog. Productized onboarding (less customer-edited catalog) would have helped.

customers.name: Homestead Cabinet
customers.evidence_quote: Got transferred over to clay (the new owner) but didn't connect with him. Need to get these guys on a demo. Left a voicemail and set task to call him back. [4/4/22] | Mark is saying allmoxy couldn't do 'Something' they were looking for. He couldn't remember. [11/9/21]
signals: Ownership transition (Mark → Clay) coincided with payment lapse
Prior owner cited a feature gap ('something Allmoxy couldn't do') but couldn't recall details
No CSM response captured after the new-owner handoff attempts
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-low — ownership-transition early warning. Build M&A radar trigger for HubSpot ownership changes.

customers.name: Safina Cabinetry
customers.evidence_quote: Cancellation Playbook 3/4/24: Close/Lost Reason: Not using the system. | These guys have completed most of the onboarding. enough to start running orders, but don't seem to be using the system anymore. Would most likely fall back into sales court. [2/23/23] | Not enrolling in a renewal sequence. No revenue. Moving to the CS/Retention pipeline! [10/11/22]
signals: Onboarding completed but customer never ran revenue
Multiple touchpoints over 18 months showing 'no revenue' state
Payment failed Jan 2024, briefly recovered card, then cancelled March 2024
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — onboarding gate at 90 days without first order. Should not have made it to year 2.

customers.name: Di Bosco Cabinets & Millwork Ltd.
customers.evidence_quote: Di Bosco will be moving shop locations and are potentially wanting to pause their account to cut costs during the transition. [1/12/24]
signals: Cost-cutting during physical shop relocation triggered pause request
Catalog managed via third-party affiliate (Chris Doyle) — not direct customer ownership
No follow-up notes after shop move
customers.recommended_action: Pull post-Jan 2024 emails/tasks to confirm whether they returned post-move or fully churned.

customers.name: ClosetParts
customers.evidence_quote: HubSpot company record contains zero notes/emails/tasks.
customers.recommended_action: Pre-CSM-playbook era — accept Unresponsive as default or interview Beau/Sarah on this name.

customers.name: Lane 17 Cabinet Co.
customers.evidence_quote: Alex hasn't had the bandwidth to invest into setting up Allmoxy as he needs it to function. Alex learned throughout the last year that he and his partner, Jason, need to first define their catalog offering within Microvellum so they can then use this outline to match up Allmoxy. Essentially they put the horse in front of the cart and weren't ready to actually implement Allmoxy. [2/11/25 Retention Plan Meeting]
signals: Customer requested 90-day pause; never came back
Owner explicitly said they didn't define their offering before signing
Multiple pauses (May→June, then full pause Mar 2025)
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-low — sales qualification should have flagged 'catalog not yet defined' as not-ready signal.

customers.name: Cutting Edge Cabinet Inc
customers.evidence_quote: Cancellation Playbook 8/12/24: Customer Close/Lost: Paid and Never Used. Close/Lost Reason: 'We are now using quick books for payroll. We have not been using online ordering. We have completed our contract.' | Launched Customer Playbook 2/27/24: 'No, using it as internal pricing tool... most difficult: Expectations for how they want to use Allmoxy not within current product offering.' Health: Red.
signals: Cancellation playbook explicitly tagged 'Paid and Never Used'
Customer used it only as internal pricing tool, never for customer orders
Pivoted to QuickBooks for payroll instead
customers.recommended_action: Preventable-high — same onboarding-gate pattern. Customer didn't fit Allmoxy's order-based model.

customers.name: Synergy Kitchens
